compiled_state.title: As a practitioner, /dso:preplanning reads the External Dependencies block and emits correctly-shaped stories or refuses decomposition
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
## Description

**What**: During preplanning decomposition, read the parent epic's ## External Dependencies block; for each entry with handling=claude_auto emit an automation story; for each entry with handling=user_manual emit a story tagged manual:awaiting_user whose done definitions include the instructions and verification_command (or confirmation-token prompt) from the block. Refuse to decompose any externally-shaped SC when the parent epic's block is missing or incomplete, with a diagnostic that points the user back to /dso:brainstorm.

**Why**: Advances SC3. This is the enforcement point that converts brainstorm intent into executable stories — without it, the block captured in brainstorm has no effect on the story map.

**Scope**:
- IN: Parser for External Dependencies block (conforming to Story A contract); story generator that emits automation stories for claude_auto entries and manual:awaiting_user-tagged stories for user_manual entries (with instructions and verification_command or confirmation-token prompt in DDs); refusal path with diagnostic when block missing or incomplete while externally-shaped SCs are present. Flag-gated by planning.external_dependency_block_enabled.
- OUT: implementation-plan-side tag guard (Story E); sprint-side tag filter and handshake (Story F); block rendering (Story B).

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, preplanning reads the parent epic's External Dependencies block (via the Story A contract) and emits an automation story for each claude_auto entry
  Satisfies: "/dso:preplanning reads the External Dependencies block and emits correctly-shaped stories. handling=claude_auto entries generate automation stories"
- When this story is complete, preplanning emits a manual:awaiting_user-tagged story for each user_manual entry; each such story's done definitions contain the instructions and verification_command (or confirmation-token prompt when verification_command is omitted)
  Satisfies: "handling=user_manual entries generate stories tagged manual:awaiting_user whose done definition contains the instructions + verification_command (or confirmation-token prompt) from the block"
- When this story is complete, preplanning refuses to decompose any externally-shaped SC when the parent epic's External Dependencies block is missing or incomplete; the refusal diagnostic names the SC and directs the user to /dso:brainstorm
  Satisfies: "Preplanning refuses to decompose any externally-shaped SC when the block is missing or incomplete."
- When this story is complete, with the flag off the block-reader and refusal paths never activate; preplanning decomposition runs exactly as before
  Satisfies: "The entire feature sits behind a config flag ... When off, all four skills behave identically to current baseline."
- When this story is complete, unit tests written and passing for block parsing, story emission (both handling variants), and the refusal path

## Considerations

- [Maintainability] Tag namespace: manual:awaiting_user coexists with design:awaiting_import (sprint already filters) and design:awaiting_review (pull-back). Document combination semantics in Story H (docs) — preplanning must permit multiple awaiting_* tags on a single story; Story F's filter logic must OR them so either tag holds the story out of the autonomous batch
- [Testing] Refusal diagnostic must be testable via preplanning skill harness without reaching user prompt
- [Maintainability] All block parsing logic references Story A contract; when the contract evolves, preplanning's reader follows
- [Reliability] Idempotent re-invocation: running preplanning on an epic whose block has already produced stories should detect existing manual:awaiting_user-tagged children and not duplicate them

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.
